Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class CharacterRomProvider

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Loads Apple IIe enhanced character ROM from embedded resources and provides glyph access.

public class CharacterRomProvider : ICharacterRomProvider
Inheritance
object
CharacterRomProvider
Implements
ICharacterRomProvider
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.MemberwiseClone()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Resource: Loads "a2e_enh_video.rom" (2048 bytes) from assembly manifest resources at construction. The character ROM contains 256 character definitions, each 8 bytes tall.

Performance: Character ROM is cached in memory for fast O(1) glyph access. All GetGlyph() calls return zero-copy views into the cached ROM buffer.

Thread Safety: Thread-safe for reads after construction. The ROM buffer is immutable and never modified after initialization.

Constructors

CharacterRomProvider()

Initializes a new instance and loads the Apple IIe enhanced character ROM.

public CharacterRomProvider()

Exceptions

InvalidOperationException

Thrown if the embedded ROM resource "a2e_enh_video.rom" cannot be found in the assembly. This typically indicates a build configuration issue where the ROM file was not embedded.

Methods

GetGlyph(byte, bool, bool)

Gets the character ROM data for a specific character.

public ReadOnlySpan<byte> GetGlyph(byte ch, bool flashOn, bool altChar)

Parameters

ch byte

The character code (0-255) to retrieve. This is the Apple II screen code (not ASCII), which determines the glyph appearance based on inverse, flashing, and alternate character set attributes.

flashOn bool

True if flash mode (inverse video) is currently active. When true, flashing characters (codes 0x40-0x7F) are displayed in their inverse state. Flash typically alternates at approximately 2 Hz.

altChar bool

True if the alternate character set (MouseText) should be used. MouseText provides graphical symbols for user interfaces, replacing control characters in the range 0x40-0x5F when enabled via the SETALTCHAR soft switch.

Returns

ReadOnlySpan<byte>

A read-only span of 8 bytes representing the character glyph, where each byte represents one horizontal scanline from top to bottom. Bits 0-6 of each byte represent pixels (1=lit, 0=dark), with bit 6 being the leftmost pixel. Bit 7 is ignored.

Remarks

The character ROM layout is organized by display attributes, not ASCII values:

  • 0x00-0x1F: Control characters (displayed as inverse uppercase when altChar is false)
  • 0x20-0x3F: Inverse special characters and numbers
  • 0x40-0x5F: Flashing uppercase letters (or MouseText when altChar is true)
  • 0x60-0x7F: Flashing special characters
  • 0x80-0x9F: Control characters displayed as normal uppercase
  • 0xA0-0xBF: Normal special characters and numbers
  • 0xC0-0xDF: Normal uppercase letters
  • 0xE0-0xFF: Normal lowercase letters and special characters
